Matías Caño, otherwise known as Pamplona, is a supporting character who appears in the Bank of Spain robbery in Parts 3, 4 and 5.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He helps the gang out with melting and storing the gold and also guards the hostages and has been an associate of Bogotá for six years.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: In his first appearance in the third episode, he is 'picked' as one of the 'volunteers' to work in the gold production area.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He refuses to do it by saying he is 'no volunteer' which angers Nairobi and results in Bogotá dragging him downstairs to the other hostages' horror.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: It is later revealed that was all an act and that he is in on the heist and one of the undercover members hired to help the main gang out.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He spends the majority of Part 3 working in the gold shaft along with Nairobi, Bogotá and the other volunteers.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He also assists the gang in helping to recover the Governor.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: In Part 4, he is now guarding the hostages more and that is his main role while the main gang can focus on producing the gold and keeping guard on any threats.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He stays downstairs with the hostages and stands in front of them, holding his gun at all times.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: When Arturo Román caused a commotion amongst the hostages, Matías approached him to warn him but was disarmed by Arturo.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: However, Manila, one of the hostages, revealed herself to have been an undercover agent of the gang, and almost immediately disposed of Arturo by shooting him in the leg and lending Matías his gun back.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: In Part 5, Matías expresses that he wants to have a code name of a city, so that in case the heist goes wrong and they're all arrested, he will be known by the name of a city, like all the other robbers, and not sound like an intern.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He chooses Pamplona, which the other gang members don't take seriously, but he insists that Pamplona is a great name.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He explains that Pamplona has the wildest festival in the world, the Festival of San Fermín, which is watched by people from around the world.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: Even the famous author Ernest Hemingway wrote the book The Sun Also Rises about American and British expatriates who travel Pamplona to watch the running of the bulls and the bullfights at the Festival of San Fermín.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: Seeing his insistence, the other robbers accept his code name and Tamayo refers to him as Pamplona when he speaks to the public.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He is quite young, probably in his mid 20s and has short brown hair and hooped ear piercings.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He wears the red jumpsuit along with everyone else and is equipped with a real gun.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He is shown to be a hard-working, loyal and funny guy.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: Matías and Bogotá have been associates for 6 years.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: However, their relationship doesn't appear to be amicable.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: Bogotá was angry at Matías for pretending not to comply with orders in front of other hostages.

title: Matías Caño
sentence_text: He reveals that he has wanted to slam Matías' head against a wagon 6 times.

The Royal Mint of Spain, also known as the Fábrica Nacional de Moneda y Timbre (National Factory of Coins and Stamps), is a government-owned institution responsible for producing and distributing currency and stamps in Spain.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: The mint has a long history, dating back to the 16th century when King Felipe II established the Casa de la Moneda (House of Coins) in Madrid.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: Today, the Royal Mint of Spain is a modern facility that uses state-of-the-art technology to produce coins and other currency items.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: It is also responsible for creating and producing official seals, documents, and stamps, including the highly-regarded Spanish postage stamps.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: The Royal Mint can produce up to €2.4 billion in the span of 128 hours, depending on various factors such as the type and denomination of the money being produced.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: The building itself is located in Madrid and is an imposing neoclassical structure with a grand entrance and an expansive interior.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: Over the years, the building has been renovated and expanded to accommodate modern production processes and security measures, though none of it was enough to stop the robbers from infiltrating and taking multiple hostages.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: The actual Royal Mint of Spain's building is not in Money Heist, but instead the Spanish National Research Council.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: In 2016, it was the location of a heist, where a group of robbers who took 67 hostages while staying inside the bank for 11 days in the Royal Mint of Spain Heist.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: They printed €984 million while in the mint.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: During the heist, the robbers force some hostages to dig a tunnel as a red herring, while Moscow digs into an already built tunnel from one of the vaults, connecting to the professor's hangar.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
sentence_text: This tunnel is the robber's intended escape route.

Mónica Gaztambide, otherwise known as Stockholm (  Spanish:   Estocolmo ), was working as the secretary of the Royal Mint of Spain until she was taken hostage by the robbers.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She fell in love with Denver, who looked after her when she was hidden in a vault.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She later became a robber in parts 3, 4 and 5 when she decided to escape with the gang.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Mónica is well-educated and learns quickly.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She only took 6 weeks to prepare for the entrance exams to get a job at the Royal Mint of Spain.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: While working as a secretary there, she begins an affair with Arturo Román.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: At the time of the heist, Mónica was pregnant with Arturo Román's child.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: At the start of the heist she is in the storage facility with Arturo.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She had just told Arturo she was pregnant, and the two got into an argument.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: In the middle of the fight, the Robbers of the Royal Mint of Spain take them hostage.

links.anchor_text: Robbers of the Royal Mint of Spain

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: When the robbers take over the mint, they close the security door and make Monica tell people outside the Royal Mint of Spain that it is a technical malfunction.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Early during the heist, Berlin tells the hostages that they will request any medicine the hostages need, and Monica requests an abortion pill.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Later, Arturo asks her to grab the phone hidden in his jacket pocket located in his office.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: When Monica goes to accept her pill, she is able to sneak past Oslo to grab the phone.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: When she successfully retrieves it, she hides it in her red jumpsuit.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: As she leaves the room, she is stopped by Denver.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver tries to talk Mónica out of aborting her child, even giving her money for the child.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Berlin interrupts the two of them and as Monica leaves the phone unexpectedly goes off.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Berlin orders Denver to execute her.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver brings Mónica to the mint's bathroom where she begs for her life.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver struggles to kill her, missing his first gun shot.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: The two of them then devise a plan to keep Monica alive.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Monica tells Denver to shoot her in the leg, as it will bleed a lot.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver follows through and shoots her in the leg to fake her death.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Berlin checks to see if the job is done, and Monica is able to fool him.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Monica tells Denver he can hide her in Vault 2, a vault she has access to.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Mónica remains hidden in the vault for a while with the bullet lodged inside her leg.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver works to help heal the wound on her leg, but Monica initially refuses to allow Denver to remove the bullet.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Upon hearing about his son "murdering" a woman, Moscow becomes distressed and blamed himself for getting Denver involved in the heist.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: When Moscow goes to get air with some of the hostages, Arturo Román is accidentally shot.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: After this, Moscow discovers Monica is alive with Denver.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: He realizes that they need to remove the bullet with a scalpel.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Upon hearing of Arturo's injury, Monica gives Denver a note to give to Arturo while he goes to steal a scalpel from the surgeon brought in to work on Arturo.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: As Denver steals the scalpel, he decides he can't give Arturo the note and accidentally drops it.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Nairobi notices the note, and follows Denver to Vault 2.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: There she discovers Monica and helps Moscow and Denver remove the bullet and patch Monica up.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: After having healed, Monica leaves the vault after feeling too confined.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver is initially mad, but realizes he can't keep her in the vault forever, so he brings her to the mint's bathroom.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: There, Berlin speaks to Denver, when Berlin realizes Denver is not alone, and discovers Monica is alive.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Berlin is a mix of angry and happy with Monica's survival, and he brings her to the break room to show the professor there have been no actual hostage casualties.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: As Arturo is recovering, he is shown that Monica is actually still alive, but their relationship is not as it was before.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Eventually after Monica has spent some time with the other hostages, she requests to be moved back to Vault 2 and Denver follows the request.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: As she spends time in the vault with Denver, the two grow closer.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver shows Monica his dance moves and tells her of his history.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: The two then start a romantic relationship.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Later, Helsinki brings Arturo Román down to the vault to see Monica.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: He was to see her about escaping with 16 other hostages.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo sees Denver and Monica's romance and challenges Denver to a fight over Monica.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Monica leaves the room as they fight.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: After the escape of 16 hostages, Monica chooses to stay in the Royal Mint of Spain with Denver instead of choosing 'freedom'.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Later, Moscow and Nairobi find out about their relationship.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: They tell Denver that it is not real love, that her "love" for him is out of fear, and it is just "Stockholm Syndrome".

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: With this information, Denver ends the romance.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Later, Monica is working with Alison Parker, Mercedes Colmenar, and Arturo Román.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: As the group is bagging money, Arturo comes up with a plan.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Alison told Arturo that the security doors are able to open in 4 seconds.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo realizes that they need a weapon if they are able to get out.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo sneaks into another room and takes one of the fake guns the robbers have and give it to Monica.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo asks Mónica to swap Denver's gun with a fake one when he cleans her wounds.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: With Denver and Monica's relationship being strained, she has him turn his back while she changes.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: In that moment, Monica steals Denver's gun and replaces it with the fake one.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Monica then gives the gun to Arturo for the escape.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Monica sits in the museum area of the Mint with Arturo, Mercedes, Alison, and some of the other hostages waiting for Helsinki to leave Denver alone guarding the hostages.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Then, the group of hostages made their way to the door until they face off with Denver.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo takes Denver hostage with the real gun, and then the other robbers arrived.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo ordered Monica to open the security door, however, Rio locks the door with a program.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Arturo threatens to shoot Denver unless him and the other hostages with him are let out.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Mónica, who still has feelings for Denver, couldn't bear to watch him die.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She runs up to Arturo and knocks him out, saving Denver.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: This makes Arturo see Monica as just one of the robbers, not as a hostage.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: From then on, she becomes an outsider to the other hostages.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Sometime later, Monica comes across Ariadna Cascales in the bathroom, where they have a conversation about love and the robbers.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: In this conversation, Ariadna confesses to Monica that she despises Berlin completely.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Their conversation only ends when they are interrupted by Nairobi.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: When Moscow is shot, Monica helps Denver and Rio as they dig the tunnel to the Professor's hanger.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She is there with Denver supporting him through him coming to terms with the fact his father might die.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: When Moscow is about to die, Monica is the one who convinces Denver to go see his father.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Monica is at Oslo and Moscow's funeral.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She pays her respects while Denver says a prayer.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She also meets The Professor when he makes his way through the completed tunnel.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She is accepted by the Professor into the robber's family.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: While Monica and Denver are speaking in the storage facility, the police break through and start their onslaught on the robbers.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: During the shootout, she grabs a dropped gun starts to shoot at the oncoming police, despite never being taught how to shoot or hold a gun before.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She escapes the room with Denver, Rio, and Tokyo, and they make their way to the escape tunnel.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: At the end of the heist, she escapes with Denver and the other robbers.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Mónica and Denver are living together in Indonesia, where Mónica gave birth to her son Cincinnati.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: They reunite with the other robbers when The Professor asks them to meet up to discuss their next heist.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: They attend classes, and Mónica prepares for her first heist as a robber.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She goes under the alias "Stockholm", a reference to her "Stockholm Syndrome".

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: The night before the heist, Mónica and Denver argue.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver is worried about her ability to carry out a heist, as she has little criminal experience.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: He tells her that she should stay behind to take care of her son.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: However, Mónica thinks Denver is being sexist and joins the heist anyway.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She stops talking to Denver for a few days.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: During the heist, Mónica is harassed by Arturo, but she stands up to him and points a gun at him.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Later, Mónica witnesses the way Denver intimidates a hostage and had second thoughts about their relationship.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: This angers Denver and makes him distance himself from her.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: After Rio returns, she supports him through his PTSD, but this upsets Denver as he thinks that they are being romantic.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Along with the other robbers, she tries to take out Gandía when he escapes and protect Nairobi.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: After Nairobi's death, she  takes over the gold smelting factory.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: During a shootout, Mónica shoots Arturo.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver admits to Mónica that he often partied while they were living in Indonesia.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Mónica reveals that she was already aware of this but did not confront him.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Denver agrees that in the future, he will be honest with her when he goes out.

sentence_text: Mónica has curly blonde hair.

sentence_text: She is tall and skinny.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: She originally was quite timid and could easily be taken advantage of, but as she joined the group and became une robber in the Bank of Spain, she got more confident and Méchante and is not afraid to boss a few hostages around and use a gun.

sentence_text: She is kind and hard-working and is always looking out for everyone in the group

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: During the heist, Denver made her realise how horrible Arturo really was and the pair quickly fell in love and as she escaped with Denver, Arturo has never met his child who was later named Cincinnati.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: During the Bank of Spain Heist, Arturo runs in the Bank of Spain when he finds out Mónica was one of the robbers.

sentence_text: Inside, he tries to pressure Mónica to let him see his son, she agrees reluctantly.

sentence_text: In Part 1, Denver meets Mónica, the secretary and mistress of Arturo Román.

sentence_text: When Berlin discovers that Mónica hid a cell phone in her clothes, he orders Denver to kill her.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: However, Denver is unable to kill her.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: He shoots her in the leg instead and hides her in a vault.

sentence_text: They spend significant time together while Mónica's wound heals.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: They soon fall in love and start a relationship, which Nairobi describes as "Stockholm Syndrome".

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Mónica becomes loyal to the crew and escapes with Denver after the heist ends.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: Between Part 2 and Part 3, while Denver and Mónica are hiding in Indonesia, Monica gives birth to Arturo's son Cincinnati and Denver becomes his step-father.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: In Part 3, Mónica joins the crew under the name "Stockholm", however Denver is reluctant to let her take part.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: He felt that she was should stay behind to take care of Cincinnati due to her lack of experience in heists.

title: Mónica Gaztambide
sentence_text: However, Mónica felt that Denver was being sexist and joins the heist at the Bank of Spain anyway.

Royal Mint of Spain Heist is a heist that took place from Friday October 21, 2016 around 10 a.m. to Wednesday October 26, 2016 at 6:32 p.m.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: On October 21, 2016 at around 10 a.m., eight robbers entered the Royal Mint of Spain, following the Professor's orders.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Their goal is to print €2.4 billion in small denominations of 50 euros, in less than eleven days, and that without shedding a single drop of blood.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: However, they will have to watch over sixty-seven hostages.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The mastermind of this plan was Jesús Marquina, the Professor's father.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: By recruiting his eight robbers, the Professor explains from the start that he does not want names, personal questions, or intimate relationships between the robbers: NPPRP (Ningún Nombre, Preguntas Personales, o Relaciónes Personales).

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The goal is to hold up the Royal Mint of Spain in Madrid to print their own untraceable banknotes without shedding the single drop of blood in order to remain "Robin Hoods".

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor hopes to print €2.4 billion.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: On Friday October 21, 2016 at 8:35am, the robbers are travelling in a van wearing Dali masks.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: At around 9:25am October 21, 2016, the eight robbers intercept the convoy delivering the watermarked paper to the mint while it was on the Carretera Commercal E-80.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The policemen, Javi and Rafita are held at gunpoint and forced to hand over their police uniform which the robbers then wear.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: They put on red jumpsuits and are put in the back of the truck.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: This allows the robbers to enter the mint discretely while being escorted by police.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: At 10:16am, the robbers make their way to the Royal Mint.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Once inside the building, they pretend that they are committing a typical heist.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: They activate the alarm and walk out with a couple of bags of bills, pretending to be surprised when the police arrive.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: However, this is all part of an act.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The robbers did not intend to rob the mint, instead, they intended to buy enough time to print €2.4 billion.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor decides to begin the heist on October 21, 2016, as it is the day that Brighton College would visit the Royal Mint of Spain Heist.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: One of the students, Alison Parker (nicknamed "Little Lamb") is of interest to the robbers as she is the daughter of the Ambassador of the United Kingdom.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: They planned on using her to give them more bargaining power when negotiating with police.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: This also pressured the police to try to forcibly enter the mint to maintain good diplomatic relations.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Raquel was against the idea of entering the mint, however, Colonel Prieto explains that these are orders from above, as rescuing Alison Parker is a priority.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: When the police attempted to enter, they drilled a small hole and inserted a small camera.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: To their surprise, they saw robbers inside, wearing masks and armed with machine guns, waiting for them to enter.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The robbers also force Alison to say on video that all hostages are dressed in red jumpsuits, just like the robbers.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: This makes police hesitant about entering the mint.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Raquel is angry at Colonel Prieto for going ahead with the plan to attempt to enter the mint, and decides that she longer wants to be on the case.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: After this, the hostages begin printing bank notes at the rate of €8 million per hour.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: On Saturday at 06:25am, 20 hours into the heist, Raquel Murillo meets the Professor for the first time at the Bar Hanoi.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He lends her his phone so she can call her mother.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: She also leaves Superintendent Sánchez a voice message to inform him that she no longer wishes to be on the case.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: By morning, all streets downtown were closed and all TV channels broadcasted the heist live for 24 hours.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Mónica Gaztambide, accompanied by 6 other people, all wearing Dali Masks and red jumpsuits come out the entrance of the mint.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Mónica takes off her mask and speaks into a megaphone, making a statement on behalf of the robbers.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: She asks the police not to shoot and reveals that there are 67 hostages in perfect health and well cared for.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: She also explains that the hostages are dressed exactly the same of the robbers, so any attempts of assault could cost lives.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Back at the police station, an officer is able to identify that the person holding the megaphone for Mónica is a robber.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: They ask for permission to shoot, but Ángel tells them not to.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Berlin looks for hostages to help with a task, and picks Pablo Ruiz, Arturo Román and one other man.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Some of the hostages request for medication.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Sonsoles Alvarez is distressed and asks for tranquilizers to calm her down.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Another hostage requests medication for diabetes, Ariadna Cascales asks for Sertraline and Mónica Gaztambide asks for an abortion pill.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Alison Parker asks the robbers to help delete an image from the internet, however it is out of his hands.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Berlin offers to let all the hostages send a video message to their families.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Moscow brings the chosen hostages to a basement and asks them to break the concrete ground along a dotted line.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He tells them they can dig up to 48 metres underground before they get to a sewer pipe.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Moscow gives them 3 hours to complete the task.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: They later discover petrol, ammunition and explosives while looking around the basement.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Alison tries to record a video with her phone to send to her family, but is too distressed to continue.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: While Alison takes a break, Tokyo and Rio get into an argument.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: While Rio is distracted, Alison tries to delete a photo from her social media account.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The police hack into the cameras on Alison's phone and sees the phones taped onto the wall.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Rio rushes over when he sees Alison with her phone and throws it away, inadvertently showing his face to the police while doing so.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: After Raquel returns to the case, the police show her the footage from Alison's phone.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Over the phone, the Professor asks the police to deliver the medication that is requested by the hostages, but Raquel offers to give a reduced sentence if he ends the heist immediately.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: She lets him know that she is serious by revealing that she has seen the picture of phones taped to the wall.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor calls Berlin who confirms that there are no security cameras in the building.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He concludes that the picture must have been taken by a hostage and orders Berlin to find the phone.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Berlin asks for permission to execute the hostage who is responsible for the photo, but the Professor refuses.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Downstairs, Arturo tells Mónica about the explosives that he found and urges her to find his phone in his office and call the police.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Berlin interrogates the hostages and asks whoever leaked the image to step forward.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Rio watches uncomfortably, knowing that he was responsible for the photo.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: As Berlin begins to order other robbers to strip search the hostages, Rio can't take it anymore and decides to reveal the truth.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Berlin has Oslo and Helsinki beat up Rio to teach him a lesson, angering Tokyo, who shoots at several cameras.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Using facial recognition, the police discover that prior to the heist, both Rio and Tokyo visited the mint, and they manage to uncover their real identities.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Food and medication is delivered.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: While Oslo is watching over the hostages in Arturo's office, Mónica manages to get Arturo's phone discretely.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Denver, who delivers the medication, tries to talk Mónica out of aborting her baby and offers her some cash.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: However, as Mónica walks away with cash, the phone in her pocket rings.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Berlin orders Denver to kill Mónica.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Unable to kill her, Denver shoots her in the leg to create a realistic murder scene, and hides her in Vault 2 of the mint.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Moscow is distraught after hearing that Denver has killed a hostage, and attempts to escape.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: 32 hours into the heist, Denver decides to have hostages accompany him and Moscow to the roof of the mint to get some fresh air.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: All the robbers and hostages are wearing red jumpsuits and masks, so the police are unable to identify them.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Arturo overhears Moscow and Denver's conversation about Mónica, and believes that they murdered her.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: In a fit of rage, he waves a fake gun at them, leading the police to believe that he is a robber, and they shoot him.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Prior to the heist, the Professor predicted that the police would try to enter the mint at some point.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He finds a way to turn this situation to his advantage.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: When Ángel enters the mint, the Professor spots him on the video surveillance footage and tells the robbers insert a miniature microphone into his glasses.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: This way, when Ángel returns to the police tent and speaks to other police officers, the Professor can overhear their plans and stay one step ahead throughout the heist.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: When the Professor gives the order to activate the Valencia plan, the robbers shoot rollers, while the hostages scream with all their might.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: This noise gives the impression of a shooting in the mint, alerting the police.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The goal is to force the police to react quickly without thinking.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Cameroon plan consists about being loved by the public.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor explains that people will always support the underdog.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: For example, if Cameroon plays against Brazil in a World Cup final, people will support Cameroon because they believe the Cameroonian team is no match for the Brazilian team.

title: Royal Mint of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Chernobyl plan is only to be used in desperate situations.

Bank of Spain Heist is a heist that began on April 4th, 2019 and lasted approximately seven days.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Rio is captured by Europol while he is in Guna Yala.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Tokyo meets the Professor at the Sanctuary of Truth in Thailand and tells him about the news.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: They predict that Rio is being tortured for information as the police have not released any information about his capture.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor reunites the robbers from the Royal Mint of Spain, Lisbon (former police officer Raquel Murillo) and Stockholm (Mónica Gaztambide, Denver's wife) and explains his plan to save Rio.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He recalls a plan to rob the Bank of Spain that he and Berlin discussed while they were in Italy.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: On D Day (  Spanish:   la hora cero ), €140 million is dropped over Madrid via zeppelin, resulting in chaos as people scramble to grab the falling cash.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: As the money is falling, the Professor appears on the TV screens at Callao Square.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He takes off his mask to reveal his face and tells the public that Rio has been captured.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Police are deployed to handle the situation and the First Squad of BRIPAC's Sixth Company is sent to the Bank of Spain.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: However, minutes before their arrival, the robbers show up at the Bank of Spain dressed as the military and are allowed inside the bank.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Once inside the bank, the gang guide the hostages into the main lobby, pretending to protect them as they blow up the building to make it look like an attack.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The professor discussed the plan on using the Police to move forward with the plans but Gandia then managed to protect the Governor from the Robbers and in the conflict managed to blind Palermo using a shard of Glass.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Flipper plan is enacted when someone touches the vault as it is filled with water.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: But the Plan never worked since the Governor is a key but it is enacted by one of the Robbers instead.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: As Alicia called Nairobi from outside the bank She brings in a child whom she thought was her son.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Alicia then order a sniper to shot Nairobi severely crippling her.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Soldiers were brought to the bank to end the robbery.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The soldiers were led by Sagasta and the team included Arantxa Arteche, Ramiro Vázquez, César Gandía, Mateo Canalejas, Salvador Torrecilla, Lucas Hernando and Isaac Cobo.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: A grenade belonging to Gandía killed Mateo Canalejas and injured Ramiro Vázquez.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Salvador Torrecilla, Lucas Hernando, Isaac Cobo, Cesar Gandia later died as well thanks to Tokyo.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: After hearing that so many solders had died, Luis Tamayo wanted to abort the mission, since the only remaining soldiers were Sagasta, Arteche and Vázquez, but Sagasta is adamant about staying in the bank.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Tatiana and Rafael, who were denied Berlin's inheritance, arrange for a group of people pretending to be police officers to intercept the robbers while they are transporting the gold.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The robbers surrender and are arrested.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: However, after being stuck in a police van for some time with no sounds of cars, they break out and discover that their gold has been stolen.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Tatiana and Rafael bury the gold in the ground and cover the land with a house and fake lawn.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Sagasta negotiates with the robbers and asks for a surgeon to treat Ramiro Vázquez.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He hands over the dog tag necklaces of the dead soldiers, including Arteche's, leading the gang to believe that she is dead as well.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The robbers agree, not knowing that the surgeons are members of the military.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Arteche crawls around the air vents in the bank and is guided by Ángel Rubio.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: When she reaches the lobby, she flashes her headlight to let Sagasta and Ramiro know to begin the attack.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Ramiro Vázquez pretends to have a seizure and the surgeons urge Palermo to handcuff them so they can give him emergency surgeyr.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He reluctantly agrees and at this moment, Arantxa Arteche jumps from behind him and pins him onto the ground.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Palermo orders Helsinki to shoot at the them, but Helsinki, being bedridden due to a broken leg, knew he would not be able to overpower the soldiers and surgeons who were point their guns at him.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Solders from outside break in, arresting all of the robbers one by one.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The robbers sporting pink jumpsuits and masks are all rounded up in the lobby, handcuffed.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Tamayo calls the Professor to let him know of the end of the heist.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor decides to turn himself in, as he promised that in the case his gang members got arrested, he would join them.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The police interrogate the gang members but get no information about the location of the gold, as none of them knew where it was.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: However, the police were still doubtful and demanded for an answer.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Though offered a free life if they revealed the gold's location, Denver refuses to take it, sending him into the police's custody.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: One by one, the rest of the gang members are also interrogated by Tamayo, with none of them giving any answers.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Meanwhile, as the police were on the search for the real gold, they are misled by some sketches found in the Professor's hideout, into thinking the gold was somewhere in the sea.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Alicia pursues her search for the real gold against time.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: The Professor, in one last stand, attempts to negotiate with Tamayo, showing him a truck outside the bank supposedly containing the gold.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He immediately gets furious realising they were gold-coated brass ingots rather than real gold, and starts counting down to killing all the heist members .

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Desperate, The Professor guilt-trips him using the economic crisis Spain had been facing, and if Tamayo killed them, the fake gold would be exposed to the public and the recession would get worse.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Tamayo and his army fire a barrage of gunshots, seemingly wiping out the whole gang.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: He goes out to announce their names and aliases, the gang being transported out of the bank in body bags.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Once inside a van, they unzip themselves and are transported to a military base.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Knowing they would never have to rob a bank again, the gang rejoices even though not everyone had survived.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: Just then Alicia comes in and exchanges a handshake with the Professor.

title: Bank of Spain Heist
sentence_text: She tells them that the gold is in Portugal.

Episode 1 (Part 1)
Welcome to the Spectacle of Life





Agustin Ramos, known by his code name Moscow (  Spanish:   Moscú ), was one of the main protagonists in Part 1 and 2 of Money Heist.

links.anchor_text: Welcome to the Spectacle of Life

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He was one of the eight robbers who went in to rob the Royal Mint of Spain and the father of Denver, another protagonist in the show.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: His first job was a miner in Asturias.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: The job caused him to get silicosis, but what caused him to quit was claustrophobia, as he couldn't stand being in a small hole.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: [3] He later became a thief and was sent to prison several times.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Little is known about Moscow's childhood, but we find out in Part 2 that when his son, Denver, was very young, his wife was struggling with a drug addiction.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow revealed that he left his wife at a roundabout to pick up her drugs, but left with Denver before she returned, and moved elsewhere.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He explains that he regrets that decision and went back many times to try and find her, but never did.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He only told Denver the truth about this during Part 2; until then Denver thought his mother left them.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Before beginning his criminal life of burglary, he used to be a miner, but it was revealed that he was claustrophobic and so left shortly after.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow was also involved in other robberies before the Royal Mint robbery, and had served time before as seen in Part 1.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He accepted the Professor's offer for the Royal Mint robbery to help Denver out after 800 pills he was holding for a dealer got stolen from his car, and help pay off his debt.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow is a fairly tall, stoutly built, bearded man.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He has gentle features, greying hair and dark brown eyes.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He is usually casually dressed, when he is not wearing the classic red jumpsuit and Dali mask.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow is a very gentle soul who cares a lot for others.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He plays a sort of fatherly figure for not only Denver, but also Rio and Tokyo.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He cares infinitely for Denver and is very protective of him as seen in Part 2, when he warns Denver about the relationship he is developing with Mónica Gaztambide.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: We also see that he is not afraid to voice the truth and his opinions when he needs to, like when he approached Tokyo after he saw her confront Alison Parker.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He accuses her of always hurting the people closest to her and having the need to always move on.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He tells her that she is probably already getting tired of Rio and is going to hurt him too.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Despite his occasionally up-front personality, his personality is mostly defined by his caring and gentle nature, particularly with his son.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He is shown to be reasonable and have a good moral compass.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He is also shown to have a happy and fun side, even ambitious as we see it's his goal to release his song 'Maria, Mi Amor' (which eventually gets released by Part 3).

title: Moscow
sentence_text: All crew members liked Moscow very much and accepted him as one of their own.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: He and his son Denver were always the ones to introduce a sense of friendship and community, whether it be through singing or dancing together during lunch at the Toledo estate.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow had a very friendly and sweet relationship with his son, and Denver looked up to him as a father, even though he wasn't an ideal role model.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Denver and Moscow have done heists together.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow describes their relationship as "just like the Sacapuntas Duo, but with burglaries."

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Denver originally wasn't supposed to be part of the heist plan, but after Moscow finds out that Denver is in debt and owed money to violent drug dealers, he talks the Professor into allowing Denver to take part in the heist.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow's goal was to earn enough money for him and Denver to live in a country whose name they can't even pronounce and spend their days with drinking mojitos by beaches.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: After hearing that Denver killed a hostage, Moscow is distraught and believes that by bringing his son to the heist, he turned his son into a murderer.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow took on a fatherly figure to Tokyo also.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: She grew fond of the old man and admired his fatherly personality.

title: Moscow
sentence_text: Moscow has been rude to Tokyo before, saying that she was the kind of person that “jumps over a river and leaves the rocks falling behind her”, although he later came to regret that he said this.

Martín Berrote, also known by his alias, Palermo, is one of the main characters in the Netflix series Money Heist, portrayed by actor Rodrigo de la Serna.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He is born in Buenos Aires, in Argentina and has known Berlin for at least 10 years.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Prior to the second heist, he had lived in Palermo, Italy.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He is a trained engineer.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Although he was not part of the Royal Mint of Spain heist, he was an acquaintance of Berlin and The Professor prior to that.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He collaborated with Berlin for coming up with the Bank of Spain heist and they both presented the plan to The Professor, who was uncertain about it.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: The Professor had his doubts about Palermo, claiming he cared more about his love for Berlin — with whom he exchanged a kiss — than the plan itself.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: After the robbery of the Royal Mint, when the crew needed a way to rescue Rio from captivity, The Professor started organizing the Bank of Spain heist, bringing Palermo into the fold at a time when he was in pain due to the loss of Berlin.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Palermo was the man in charge at the bank, but his volatile personality soon caused tensions with rest of the group and Tokyo assumed command.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Palermo became a rival for the crew for some time and was responsible for talking Gandía, the bank's head of security, into freeing himself.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: However, he was eventually reintegrated into the group.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: During the heist, he was shot at by Gandía and he ended up needing surgery to get shards of glass removed from his eyes.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He wore an eye patch until Part 4 Episode 2.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Palermo has brown hair.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He is not very tall.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He wore an eye patch for much of heist at the Bank of Spain due to an injury.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He is a complex, skilled engineer with egoistical tendencies.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He needs to be in charge and doesn't tolerate being out of control very well.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Being an impulsive character, he sometimes makes decisions without thinking them through, leading to unwanted complications.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Nevertheless, he is able to admit his wrongdoings and willing to fix the problems they caused if possible.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: The first impression people get of him is that of a cold, mean and uncaring personality.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: It takes some time to realize that this is just the façade he has built for himself in order not to get hurt.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Furthermore, he is passionate about the things he wants.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: He displays this for the heist, as it was made by Berlin, the man who meant the most to him and loved dearly.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Palermo was in love with his best friend Berlin for ten years, so much so that he suffered severely from depression after his death, turning to alcohol as a coping mechanism.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: During an argument, Nairobi accuses Palermo of being so weak that he has never been able to tell Berlin that he is in love with him.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: During the planning of the second heist, Palermo and Helsinki developed a physical relationship.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Due to his heartbreak from Berlin, Palermo decided not to let anyone else come close emotionally, including Helsinki, whereas Helsinki had supposedly fallen in love with him.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Whether Palermo loved him back is not given much attention.

title: Palermo
sentence_text: Later on in the show, Helsinki manages to break through his façade as their relationship grows.

sentence_text: Money Heist (also known as La Casa de Papel) is a Spanish television crime-drama series.

title: Money Heist Wiki
sentence_text: An enigmatic man, nicknamed The Professor, plans the biggest heist in history.

title: Money Heist Wiki
sentence_text: To carry out this plan, he recruits eight of the country's top criminals who have nothing to lose: Tokyo, an experienced robber, Berlin, the ringleader, Moscow, the drilling expert, Rio, the computer scientist, Nairobi, the counterfeiter, Denver, son of Moscow, and Helsinki and Oslo, the Balkan war veterans.

title: Money Heist Wiki
sentence_text: The goal is to infiltrate the Royal Mint of Spain in order to print €2.4 billion in less than 11 days, without spelling a single drop of blood.

title: Money Heist Wiki
sentence_text: The group takes 67 hostages, including the daughter of the British Ambassador.

title: Money Heist Wiki
sentence_text: With the mastermind working on the outside, they always manage to stay one step ahead of the police.

Part 1 (also referred to as the first part of season 1) of Netflix's Money Heist consisted of 13 episodes, and  aired on December 20, 2017.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: Eight thieves take hostages and lock themselves in the Royal Mint of Spain as a criminal mastermind manipulates the police to carry out his plan.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: Part 1 begins with the aftermath of a failed bank robbery by a woman named "Tokyo", as a man named the "Professor" saves her from being caught by the police and proposes to her a heist of drastic proportions.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: After a brief outline of the planned heist, the story jumps to the beginning of a multi-day assault on the Royal Mint of Spain in Madrid.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: The eight robbers are code-named after cities: Tokyo, Moscow, Berlin, Nairobi, Rio, Denver, Helsinki and Oslo.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: Dressed in red jumpsuits with a mask of the Spanish painter Salvador Dalí, the group of robbers take 67 hostages as part of their plan to print and escape with €2.4 billion through a self-built escape tunnel.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: The Professor heads the heist from an external location.

title: Part 1
sentence_text: Flashbacks throughout the series show the five months of preparation in an abandoned hunting estate in the Toledo countryside; the robbers are not to share personal information nor engage in personal relationships, and the assault shall be without bloodshed.

sentence_text: She makes a last call to her mother before leaving the country.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: A stranger approaches her in the street and proposes that she join his heist - where he plans to print €2.4 billion.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: She accepts, and finds herself with seven other robbers in a house in Toledo, where a man named the Professor prepares them for the heist for five months.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: To remain anonymous, each robber chooses a city as a code-name, and she becomes Tokyo.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: She will carry out this heist alongside Berlin, a jewelry thief, Moscow, a specialist in mechanics, and his son Denver, who has a penchant for fighting.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: She also meets Rio, a young hacker, Oslo and Helsinki, two Serbs, and Nairobi, a counterfeiter.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: The Professor announces to them that they are going to rob the Royal Mint of Spain.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: On the day of the robbery, a group of high school students from Brighton College visit the mint.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: One of the students, Alison Parker, is the daughter of the British Ambassador, who is a close friend of the Queen of England.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: At the same time, Mónica Gaztambide, an employee of the mint, announces to her lover, Arturo Román, who is none other than the director, that she is pregnant with his child.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: It is at this moment, the eight robbers hijack the truck that is responsible for delivering the paper money, and enter the premises.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: They close the armored doors from the inside and lock themselves up with sixty-seven hostages.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: They confiscate the cell phones, and install a cable to communicate with the Professor who remained in a hangar nearby.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: Meanwhile, the police hire Raquel Murillo, the country's best negotiator.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: After opening a vault, the robbers do what they had planned to do: pretend to come out, shoot in the air and re-enter.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: But all does not go as planned, as two policemen are injured and Rio is shot.

title: Episode 1 (Part 1)
sentence_text: We learn that the day before the robbery, Rio offers his dog tag necklace that bears his real name to Tokyo, hence breaking one of the Professor's rules: no personal relationships.

sentence_text: To breathe a bit, Raquel talks to Salva at the Bar Hanoi.

sentence_text: She tells him that her ex-husband beat her for several years, and that she did not dare to file a complaint because he was also a police officer and was admired by everyone the police station.

sentence_text: He explains that he had no choice and the others are completely lost, since the rule of "no blood" has been broken.

sentence_text: He decides to open the doors of the Factory to breathe some air, risking being shot by the police.

sentence_text: Denver manages to bring him in at the last moment by explaining to him that he did not actually kill the hostage, but that he was pretending.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: It aired on December 20, 2017.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: A break in the investigation and a mistake by one of the thieves puts the Professor at serious risk of being discovered.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: After watching many hours of video surveillance, the police finally find a clue that could be useful to them.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: The day Rio and Tokyo went into the Royal Mint of Spain to locate the premises, they left their car key at the entrance.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: The police research the exact model of the vehicle (from the photo of the car keys) and find that it is a Seat Ibiza from 1992.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: At the same time, the Professor, who overhears the plan of the police, calls Helsinki to confirm that he indeed had the car destroyed in a car junkyard.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: The Professor had previously given Helsinki a thousand euros to get the car destroyed.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: However, Helsinki reveals that he instead sent the money to his family.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: It is uncertain whether the vehicle is still in the junkyard, whch is dangerous and fingerprints are still on the car.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: The Professor therefore decides to go to the junkyard to find the car, but it is closed.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: He decides to enter anyway and is spotted by the guard, Nikolai Dimitrievich, who pursues him for a while.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: After he hides in an old car, the guard lifts him up with a crane.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: When he finally gets down to the ground, the Professor tries to buy his car back.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: However, as he did not have much money on him, the guard throws him out.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: Meanwhile, Berlin has isolated a few women from the rest of the group, presumably to scare them.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: Rio and Tokyo discuss what awaits them on the outside and the reasons that led them to enter the world of crime.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: When returning to the hangar, the Professor receives a call from the police who suggest that he free some hostages.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: He accepts and gives a list of 8 students ready to be released, but Alison Parker, Colonel Prieto's priority, is not on the list.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: The Professor finally agrees to release just Alison, instead of 8 hostages, which the police accept.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: Unfortunately for Raquel, he recorded the conversation and sent it to radio stations across the country to create bad publicity for the police.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: Some time later, the search for the car resumes and Raquel orders police to search the nearby scrapyards.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: Hearing this, the Professor decides to go back to the scrapyard, and begins to disinfect the car.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: He takes the opportunity to voluntarily drop a button of the Berlin jacket inside, and narrowly escapes the police who have just arrived.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: To get out of the junkyard without being noticed, he disguises himself as a beggar and manages to get back to the hangar.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: At the same time, Raquel finds the products he just used to clean the car, and realises that it is too late, as the mastermind of the heist has already left the scene.

title: Episode 7 (Part 1)
sentence_text: We learn that Tokyo's mother died of a heart attack in the months leading up to the heist.

Mirko Dragic, mostly known by his alias Helsinki, is one of the main characters in the Netflix series Money Heist, portrayed by actor Darko Perić.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: Nairobi (one-sided love on her side)

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: He is one of the robbers of the Royal Mint of Spain and the Bank of Spain.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: In the Royal Mint heist, Helsinki was mainly in charge of watching over the hostages, teasing Arturo, and chatting with Oslo.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: In the second season, he suffocated Oslo, who was in a vegetative state.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: Helsinki was born in Serbia.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: He was a soldier in the Yugoslav Wars with his cousin Oslo.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: He has a family in Serbia, to whom he sends money when he can.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: During the war, he had a bullet taken out of him in a tractor garage.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: The Professor recruits Helsinki in addition to seven other robbers, including his cousin Oslo.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: He was responsible for making the Professor's Seat Ibiza disappear after Tokyo and Rio used it to go to the Museum of the Factory.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: The Professor gave him with a thousand euros in order to pay the employees of the car junkyard to destroy it in front of him.

title: Helsinki
sentence_text: On Sunday 23 October, the Professor called the crew via the red telephone, to ask Helsinki if he had indeed destroyed the car.

sentence_text: This forces the Professor to leave his lair and go to the scrapyard in order to erase any evidence before the police arrive.

sentence_text: That's not cold-blooded — it's portrayed as an act of love and mercy coming from someone who's seen too much war trauma.

sentence_text: Arturo constantly got in trouble with the robbers.

sentence_text: The scissors did nothing to Denver, and Arturo takes completely turns from being angry and confident to scared and sorrowful.

sentence_text: He is sent back down to continue to dig the hole he had with Pablo Ruiz with 5 other hostages.

sentence_text: They report the find to Tokyo and Rio who give them a sledge hammer to continue.

sentence_text: When Arturo and the 5 other hostages get though the wall they encounter a group of armed police waiting for them guns drawn.

sentence_text: The police had believed that Arturo and the other 5 hostages were the robbers trying to escape into the sewer.

sentence_text: With this, Arturo is freed from captivity from the Royal Mint of Spain.

sentence_text: When the crew returned to Spain to rob the Bank of Spain, Arturo approached the building and ran inside when the entrance doors were about to close, becoming a hostage again.

sentence_text: He tried to manipulate other hostages to go against the robbers, much like in the previous heist.

title: Arturo Román
sentence_text: This time, however, the attempts were marred by his predatory behavior towards women; he drugged and raped Amanda, and later tried to do the same to Manila.

title: Arturo Román
sentence_text: Arturo has short brown hair.

title: Arturo Román
sentence_text: Arturo is a coward who tries to escape from severe consequences by letting others do the deeds, putting the other hostages into danger.

title: Arturo Román

title: Arturo Román
sentence_text: In addition he's a misogynistic narcissist and feels entitled to women's bodies, making use of them to satisfy his own needs by giving them sleep-inducing anxiety pills.

Radko Dragić , better known by his alias Oslo, was a secondary character in the Netflix series Money Heist, portrayed by actor Roberto García.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: He was a robber and war veteran who participated in the Royal Mint of Spain heist.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo was born on November 17th, 1974 in Belgrade, Serbia.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo fought in the Yugoslav Wars with his cousin Helsinki.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo died on October 24, 2016 at the age of 41.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo and cousin Helsinki act as hired guns and the muscle during the heist.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: They're usually seen acting as crowd control.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: They don't really think independently, and mostly just follow orders.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo and Helsinki helped Berlin to punish Rio in Part 1 Episode 3, and are also seen aiming guns at Denver and Nairobi during a standoff with Berlin in Part 1 Episode 9.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: In Part 1 Episode 12, Oslo was struck across the head with an iron rod by a hostage and 15 other hostages as part of an escape plan.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: This knocked him unconscious allowing the hostages to grab his gun and eventually escape, blowing a hole in a wall that the rest of the gang have to cover up.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo is left in a vegetative state for the rest of Part 1.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: In Part 2 Episode 1, seemingly unable to revive him, Helsinki takes Oslo to a storage room and suffocates him with a pillow, mercifully killing him.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: This makes Oslo the first robber in the show to die.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo, like his cousin Helsinki, is a large man presumably due to both their backgrounds in war.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: However, he is slightly shorter and possibly less heavyset than Helsinki.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: He has a mohawk on top of his head, and sports a long scraggly beard rather than a full one like Helsinki.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: As remarked by Tokyo after his death, Oslo was the quietest robber out of the group.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: It is unknown if this is due to an introverted nature, or due to the fact he doesn't seem to be as adept at speaking Spanish as his cousin Helsinki (who is much more social), either only understanding simple sentences, or often having to rely on him to translate orders into Serbian.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo's impaired ability to speak Spanish is first revealed during a flashback in Part 1 in which the Professor is seen conversing with him in the background about learning new languages.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo seems to have a no-nonsense attitude to work, displaying a steely-eyed look and cold demeanor.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Again, this could simply be due to the language barrier between him and the rest of the team.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo has been seen to have a lighter side however, dancing with cousin Helsinki during Moscow's song 'Maria, my love' and chuckling at Berlin's joke (admittedly, at Denver's expense) in Part 4 Episode 5.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Helsinki is Oslo's cousin, and together they fought in wars.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Helsinki and Oslo are close, having traveled and done work together for a while before they are recruited by the Professor.

title: Oslo
sentence_text: Oslo trusts Helsinki enough to request Helsinki to kill him when he is put in a vegetive state.

Part 2 (also referred to as the second part of season 1) of Netflix's Money Heist consisted of 9 episodes, and aired on April 6, 2018.

title: Part 2
sentence_text: As the police close in on the Professor's identity, his lack of communication with the Mint team leads to mutiny and the arrest of one of the robbers.

title: Part 2
sentence_text: Throughout parts 1 and 2, the robbers inside the Mint have difficulties sticking to the pre-defined rules, and face uncooperative hostages, violence, isolation, and mutiny.

title: Part 2
sentence_text: Tokyo commentates the events through voice-overs.

title: Part 2
sentence_text: While Denver pursues a love affair with the hostage Mónica Gaztambide, inspector Raquel Murillo of the National Police Corps negotiates with The Professor on the outside and begins an intimate relationship with his alter ego "Salva".

title: Part 2
sentence_text: The Professor's identity is repeatedly close to being uncovered, until Raquel realises his true identity, but is emotionally unable and unwilling to hand him over to the police.

title: Part 2
sentence_text: At the end of part 2, after 128 hours, the robbers escape successfully from the Mint with €984 million printed, but at the cost of the lives of Oslo, Moscow and Berlin.

title: Part 2
sentence_text: One year after the heist, Raquel decodes postcards left by the Professor for a location in Palawan in the Philippines, where she reunites with him.

Part 3 (also referred to as the first part of season 2) of Netflix's Money Heist consisted of 8 episodes, and aired on July 19, 2019.

sentence_text: Three years after the heist on the Royal Mint of Spain, the robbers are enjoying their lives paired-up in diverse locations.

title: Part 3
sentence_text: He and Raquel (going by "Lisbon") get the gang, including Mónica (going by "Stockholm"), back together and enlist three new members: Bogotá, Palermo and Marseille, with Palermo in charge.

title: Part 3
sentence_text: The disguised robbers sneak into the heavily guarded bank, take hostages and eventually gain access to the gold and state secrets, while the Professor and Lisbon travel in an RV and then an ambulance while communicating with the robbers and the police.

title: Part 3
sentence_text: A breach in the bank is thwarted, forcing the police, led by Colonel Luis Tamayo and pregnant inspector Alicia Sierra, to release Rio to the robbers.

title: Part 3

sentence_text: Money Heist Part 4 begins in chaos: The Professor thinks that Lisbon has been executed, Rio and Tokyo have blown up an army tank and Nairobi is struggling between life and death.

sentence_text: The gang is going through one of its toughest moments and the rise of an enemy among its ranks will put the heist in serious danger.

sentence_text: While Tokyo stages a coup d'état and takes over command from Palermo, the Professor and Marseille deduce that Lisbon must still be alive and being interrogated by Sierra in a tent outside of the bank.

sentence_text: As the group manages to save Nairobi's life, the restrained Palermo creates chaos to reestablish his command by colluding with Gandía, the restrained chief of security for the Bank of Spain.

sentence_text: Gandía escapes, begins communications with the police from within a panic room inside the bank, and participates in a violent cat-and-mouse game with the gang.

sentence_text: Gandía shoots Nairobi in the head, killing her instantly, but the gang later recapture him.

sentence_text: The Professor enlists external help to free Lisbon during her transfer to the Supreme Court.

Money Heist (also known as La Casa de Papel) is a Spanish television heist crime drama series.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: Created by Álex Pina, the series was initially intended as a limited series to be told in two parts.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: It had its original run of 15 episodes on Spanish network Antena 3 from May 2, 2017 to November 23, 2017.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: Netflix acquired the global streaming rights in late 2017.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: It re-cut the series into 22 shorter episodes and released them worldwide, beginning with the first part on December 20, 2017, followed by the second part on April 6, 2018.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: In April 2018, Netflix renewed the series with a significantly increased budget for 16 new episodes total.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: Part 3, with eight episodes, was released on July 19, 2019.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: Part 4, also with eight episodes, was released on April 3, 2020.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: Part 5, with 10 episodes, had its first five episodes released on September 3, 2021 and the last five on December 3, 2021.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: The first two parts revolve around a long-prepared, multi-day assault on the Royal Mint of Spain in Madrid, in which a group of robbers take hostages as part of their plan to print and escape with €2.4 billion.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: It involves eight robbers, code-named after cities and led by the Professor from an external location.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: In the third part, the surviving robbers are forced out of hiding, and with the help of new members, they plan and perform an assault on the Bank of Spain.

title: Money Heist
sentence_text: The fourth part continues this plot.

Strike the Tent (  Spanish:   Tumbar la carpa ) is the seventh episode of Part 4 of Netflix's Money Heist.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: It aired on April 3, 2020.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The group reels following the attack on one of their own.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The Professor puts his adjusted plan in motion with the help of new recruits and Palermo.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: In a flash forward, Gandía communicates with Tamayo that he has eliminated Nairobi and Tokyo, and that he is heading to the roof where he needs a helicopter.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: In the present, a wounded Gandía makes it back to the panic room.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The gang uncover the tunnel to the panic room behind the governor's bathroom.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Palermo informs the Professor that Nairobi has been killed.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Meanwhile, the police use robots to defuse a bomb at the front entrance of the bank.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Tamayo calls Gandía's communications centre, but the telecommunication cords are cut by Bogotá before he can answer.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The Professor tells the gang to drill a hole through to the panic room and inject narcotic gas to incapacitate Gandía before rescuing Tokyo.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The Professor gets people to put up posters all over Madrid that bear the face of Rio with the title "buried alive," and nationally broadcasts a video of Rio recounting his illegal detainment by police and his torture.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Tamayo insists that they will have to vehemently deny these accusations to the press.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Meanwhile, the Professor enlists Benjamín, father of Manila, and his crew to participate in a plan to free Lisbon.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: In a press conference, Colonel Prieto denies Rio's accusations, but Marseille forces a man who took part in Rio's torture in Algeria to corroborate Rio's story, which is broadcast nationally via video during the press conference.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: This deflates Tamayo's plans, and he calls off the assault on the bank.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Tamayo tells Sierra that she has to take the blame, and although she will be fired, she will be paid from reserve funds and promises no prison time.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: Meanwhile, Gandía unties Tokyo to remove the shrapnel from his back; she knocks him unconscious and the group frees her.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The Professor then nationally broadcasts a video of himself exposing the illegal detainment of Lisbon inside the tent outside the bank.

title: Strike the Tent
sentence_text: The Ministry of the Interior takes over Lisbon's custody, and removes her from the tent.
