The Paris Plan (  Spanish:   Plan París ) is the eighth and final episode of Part 4 of Netflix's Money Heist.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: It aired on April 3, 2020.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: The Professor, Benjamin, and the others attempt to free Lisboa.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Tokyo's anger threatens her faith in the plan.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Sierra takes matters into her own hands.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: In flashbacks, Berlin confronts Palermo about his obvious feelings towards him, and also reveals his own feelings for Palermo.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: They passionately kiss, however, Berlin says they must part ways and give up the plan because their love would interfere too much.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: In the present, Sierra admits to the press that she had used Nairobi's son to lure her to the window to be shot, that she was later killed by the chief of security of the bank, Gandía, as supported by Colonel Tamayo, and that Rio was buried alive.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Tamayo watches her televised speech in disgust.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Benjamín and Marseille go to a Chinese restaurant to begin constructing a tunnel that leads to the parking garage of the Supreme Court, where Lisbon is brought to testify.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Meanwhile, Gandía is restrained and regains consciousness.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: As Sierra is fired and legal proceedings are initiated against her, she begins a pursuit of the Professor on her own.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Sierra deduces that Antoñanzas must be a mole, and tracks down his wife.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Sierra extracts information from her that he had been found in the pool with two friends in the middle of the night.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Sierra holds the security guard of the apartment building at gunpoint in order for her to get the security camera footage, and finds the footage of Antoñanzas being helped out of the pool by the Professor, as well as the license plate of his car from the parking lot.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Meanwhile, the Professor transmits a signal of a fake parking garage so that Benjamín and his crew can drill the hole to the parking garage and rescue Lisbon without being noticed by the guards.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: At the bank, the gang forces Gandía to radio to Tamayo that he plans to begin another attack.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Meanwhile, Benjamín and his crew disguise themselves as a judge, lawyer and prisoner, and when Lisbon is escorted from the court through the parking garage, they take control of the guards.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: They strap them with bombs, bring in a woman disguised as Lisbon, and force them to take their normally scheduled route outside of the parking garage.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Lisbon crawls through the tunnel from the parking garage to the Chinese restaurant.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: As the gang fires their weapons, they force Gandía to radio to Tamayo that he is going to the roof and requests to be airlifted out.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: The Professor intercepts Tamayo's request for a helicopter, and brings his own, that was acquired on the black market, piloted by Marseille to transport Lisbon to the bank.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: The gang creates a ruse on the roof to create the impression that Gandía is fighting.

title: The Paris Plan
sentence_text: Lisbon, disguised as police, descends onto the roof, and is reunited with the gang; they cheer and chant "for Nairobi!"

Santiago López, better known by the code name Bogotá, is a character in the Netflix series Money Heist series, appearing as a main character in season 3 and season 4.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He was part of the crew who carried out the robbery at the Bank of Spain.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Not much is known about Bogotá's early life.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He has seven children around the world: Emilio (Venezuela), Julián (Mexico), Erik (Iceland), Hanna (Finland), Drazen (Montenegro), Yaris (Greece), and Ivanna (Ukraine).

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He was present at Berlin's wedding with Tatiana.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Prior to the Bank of Spain heist, He also participated in the Fredericksborg castle heist alongside Rafael, Tatiana, Marseille and Berlin which he take the role of a SCUBA diver.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: During the Bank of Spain heist, he helps Berlin in a flashback scene by setting off a small explosive in the basement of the bank, whilst Berlin is with the Governor in his office.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: In season 4, he fell in love with Nairobi.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Bogotá is the world's best welder and has a vital role in the heist at the Bank of Spain.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He is the main member in charge alongside Nairobi to lead the group that is melting the gold bricks in the national reserve into grains of gold at the Bank of Spain.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: It is through his talents of welding that the team is able to pull-off the successful extraction of the gold from the flooded vault.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: His welding and heroic efforts (placing a bomb underwater while being present in the enclosed vault, welding underwater without an oxygen tank) allow the heist to move forward as planned initially.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He is a tall man with a dad-bod type physique and dark brown hair with a slightly lighter brown beard.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He has brown eyes.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Bogotá is generally a good guy who respects his team and is willing to help his colleagues out.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He acts like a typical alpha male as he is tough and brave, but also makes slightly sexist remarks that objectify Nairobi.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: When she calls him out, he learns from this mistake.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He doesn’t like vegans much.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Bogotá believes that a good father is one that behaves exactly the same way they did before becoming a father, and he believes himself to be a good one.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He admits that he's not a present father and that he only shows up unannounced from time to time, but he claims that he takes care of all of his children.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Bogotá says that he doesn't ask them for anything but, nonetheless, they throw their arms around his neck and cover him with kisses simply because he's their father.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He keeps a photo of each of his seven children in his wallet and seems to know them to a certain degree, judging by the way he describes them to Nairobi and Denver.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Emilio is the oldest of Bogotá's children and lives in Venezuela.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Julián lives in Mexico City; he's growing a mustache and calls Bogotá "güey" (dude).

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Erik, from Iceland, who Bogotá says is funny as hell.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Hanna, a violinist, lives in Finland and is described as being a beautiful girl.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Drazen from Montenegro, who Bogotá labels as a really boring kid.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Yaris lives in Thessaloniki (Greece) and who Bogotá says is quite a character.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Ivanna is ill-tempered, lives in Odessa (Ukraine) and is an artist.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Initially, Bogotá and Nairobi banter with one another.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: When he says something sexist and Nairobi stands up for herself, he is smitten.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He falls in love with Nairobi, but Nairobi tells him she wouldn't even touch him with a 10-foot pole.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Yet, when she gets shot initially in the lung and has surgery, Bogotá is by her bedside throughout her recovery ensuring she is safe.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: While she is still asleep from the surgery, he prepares a means of transportation for her as he knows it will be difficult for her to walk and she will want to be involved in the heist still.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: This expresses how much he cares for Nairobi.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: When she wakes up, he confesses his love for her by saying he can imagine her dressed in white saying "suck dick."

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: She reciprocates these feelings and they share an intimate moment looking into one another's eyes.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Post surgery and their visit to the team working on melting the gold, Nairobi and Bogotá are alone in an elevator together.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: She teases him that he is being shy and not flirting with her anymore.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He explains he does not want to take advantage of her while she is hurt, and she teases him and asks if he still wants to be a couple.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: He proves he cares about her by simply kissing her.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: She mocks him, saying "You call that a kiss?"

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Nairobi gets up with the help of Bogotá and they share a passionate kiss in the elevator.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Bogotá continues to take care of Nairobi as Gandía is on the loose.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: However, once he has to leave her to help his fellow gang members, she is taken hostage by Gandía in the bathroom.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: Bogotá is devastated and desperately wants to save Nairobi and hopes he can with the help of his team.

title: Bogotá
sentence_text: However, their relationship doesn't appear to be amicable.

Julia Martínez, also known by her code name, Manila, is a character in the Netflix series Money Heist, portrayed by actress Belén Cuesta.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Julia joined the crew of robbers as requested by Moscow and Denver to The Professor, as they promised her father, Benjamin, that they would take care of her.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Although she was not included in the Royal Mint of Spain robbery, she did participate in the Bank of Spain heist.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Her task consists of being among the other hostages, pretending to be one of them as a form of crowd control.

title: Manila
sentence_text: She constantly fakes needing to go to the bathroom to be able to inform the robbers about any developments.

title: Manila
sentence_text: She is eager to drop the ruse and join the other robbers and eventually does so to prevent Arturo Román from revolting, shooting him in the leg.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Born as Juan, her father Benjamin is a close friend of Moscow.

title: Manila
sentence_text: She and Denver have been close friends since childhood, with Moscow mentioning how they were like siblings.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Because she is a transgender individual, she eventually transitioned as Julia, which Denver and Moscow did not know about until they saw her again.

title: Manila
sentence_text: She changed her gender, which she revealed to Denver and Moscow in Part 3.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Moscow requested that she join the gang when they were planning the robbery of the Royal Mint of Spain in Toledo.

title: Manila
sentence_text: In Part 3, Manila is just another hostage in the Bank of Spain and has no major role in the heist yet.

title: Manila
sentence_text: During this time, she is just doing her role as an undercover agent of the robbers.

title: Manila
sentence_text: She is seen multiple times observing the hostages and their activities.

title: Manila
sentence_text: In Part 4, Manila gets more to do, as she is seen watching over Arturo Román's actions.

title: Manila
sentence_text: We find out about her relationship to Denver and Moscow within flashbacks and their want to involve her in the Royal Mint of Spain heist.

title: Manila
sentence_text: We also see Denver and Moscow's positive, if not a bit confused at first, acceptance of Manila being transgender.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Back in the bank, after César Gandía's escape she and the other hostages are put together in the library.

title: Manila
sentence_text: On the way there, Arturo offers her 'anxiety' pills, the same he used to roofie Amanda, so he could rape her.

title: Manila
sentence_text: When a couple of the surrounding hostages find out and start yelling at Arturo, Matías Caño, who is watching over the hostages, goes to check out what is happening and gets ambushed by Arturo.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Arturo grabs Matías' gun, but is shot by Manila with a pistol Denver gave her earlier, revealing herself to be an undercover robber.

title: Manila
sentence_text: In Part 5, Manila confesses to Denver that she has feelings for him.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Denver tries to push her away, but when they talk, she makes Denver realized that he has much more in common with Manila than with Mónica.

title: Manila
sentence_text: Denver and Manila end up sharing a kiss.

sentence_text: The Professor appears to have exceptional knowledge in negotiation tactics, police strategy, weaponry, law enforcement, hostage crises aversion, forgery, theft and bank robberies.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He was able to control the entirety of the heist from behind the scenes, playing with the police and constantly tricking them into doing exactly what he wanted them to do.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He also demonstrates a deep understanding of human psychology.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: A majority of his plans gravely depend upon the reactions he predicts from the police, the government, and the public.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: This allows him to play a tense game of one-upmanship with Inspector Raquel Murillo, where he repeatedly manages to shift the status quo in favor of his team during the heist, despite overwhelming odds.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: His calm demeanor complements his innate leadership traits, which allows him to resolve team conflicts without a fuss.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Moreover, team members have great trust in his plans and decision-making ability.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: This is apparent when the team chooses to respect the protocol of waiting for 24 hours for his phone telephone call before activating 'Plan Chernobyl', despite clear evidence on the television of his impending imprisonment a few hours before the deadline.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: They do this as they have immense respect for his intellect and believe he will do everything in his power to save the team from any crisis.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Sergio also thinks of himself as a strategist similar to a chess-player, who needs to carefully calculate every move before arriving at a conclusion.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: This allows him to make crucial decisions in very short periods of time when situations go awry.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He may possibly be an expert martial artist, as he easily subdues Alberto and Raquel on separate occasions.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He is shown practicing boxing and undergoing intense physical training in his hideout.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Despite planning the biggest heist in human history, the Professor maintains stringent moral principles.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He refrains from killing Raquel's mother even when it appears to be the only possibility of survival for the heist.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He maintains that not spilling blood is the most important rule of the heist.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: However, it is unclear if this is only due to consideration of public opinion, or if it arises from a moral standpoint.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: He also shows great compassion and love for his team members, despite beginning his heist classes with the 'No Personal Relationships' rule.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Raquel first meets the Professor in a café near the Mint when he lends her his cell phone to call her mother.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: They become friends and eventually fall in love, unaware that he is the "mastermind" of the heist.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: When she finds out, she does everything possible to trick him and take him to jail, but he eventually convinces her and she gives in to his plan, letting him escape with the money and finding him a year later in Palawan, an island in the Philippines.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: The Professor was the first person that Tokyo meets in the crew, and was the one who saved her from spending her life in jail.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Throughout the series, Tokyo has shown to greatly admire and respect The Professor, even going so far as to call him her 'guardian angel'.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Berlin and the Professor are brothers and have a close relationship.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: In a flashback of Berlin and the Professor in Italy, Berlin reveals that he has Helmer's Myopathy, a condition that his mother had as well.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: The doctors predicted that he would have 3 years to live, but Berlin is hopeful that he will have 6 or 7 years to live.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: The Professor immediately decides to call off the heist and find treatment for him abroad, but Berlin refuses as there is no cure and he preferred to continue with his passion - robbing.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Alberto met the Professor, when he was disguised as Salva.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Alberto did not like the Professor and his relationship with Raquel.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: They first meet at The Toledo House and get into an argument when Alberto drives the Professor back to Madrid.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: Their argument culminates in a fist fight which the Professor quickly wins.

title: The Professor
sentence_text: With this, Alberto uses the chance to arrest the Professor under the assumption that he is Salva.

Andrés de Fonollosa, better known by his code name Berlin, is one of the main characters in the Netflix series Money Heist and spinoff series Berlin, portrayed by actor Pedro Alonso.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: The Professor (younger brother)Rafael (son)

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He is the second-in-command of the Royal Mint of Spain heist, but also planned the Bank of Spain heist years prior, which he shared with The Professor and Palermo.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin was born on April 7, 1975[1].

title: Berlin
sentence_text: We don’t know much about Berlin’s backstory except the fact that he was the older brother of the Professor.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: We can presume that they had a happy childhood until their father got shot during a robbery in order to pay for his brother’s treatment, who was extremly sick during this time.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Before joining the Robbers of The Royal Mint of Spain, Berlin was a jewel thief, with twenty-seven robberies to his name of jewelry stores, auction houses, and armored vehicles.

links.anchor_text: Robbers of The Royal Mint of Spain

title: Berlin
sentence_text: His biggest theft, before the Royal Mint of Spain, was 434 diamonds in Champs-Élysées, Paris.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He was later diagnosed with Helmer's Myopathy.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Somewhere in his life, he was recruited by his half brother to be the leader of the “greatest robbery ever”.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He was trained in various things along with his fellow robbers, and he learned all the details of the heist.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin has been married 5 times.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: One of his ex-wives is Tatiana.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: They divorced after Berlin discovered that Tatiana had cheated on him with his son Rafael.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Prior to the Royal Mint of Spain Heist, he managed to steal at an Auction house in Paris, France alongside: Cameron, Roi, Damian, Bruce, Sheila.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Against opponents: Raquel and Alicia.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin was the on-field leader of the Heist Crew, making decisions that were not important enough to consult with The Professor or when he couldn't answer the phone.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin was still the on-field leader, temporarily being replaced by Nairobi only to later on regain his position.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: In the end, he sacrifices himself and gets gunned down by Suárez in order to give the other robbers time to escape.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Although Berlin is dead, he shows up in flashbacks from 5 years ago in Italy with The Professor and Palermo.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: These flashbacks include him coming up with a plan, him stabbing another man in a bathroom, or his own wedding with Tatiana.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: In The Paris Plan, he is shown conversing with Palermo, which ends in them kissing.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: In flashbacks, Berlin is shown bringing his son Rafael to his first ever heist at Frederiksborg Castle in Denmark, with the assistance of his wife Tatiana, Marseille and Benjamín Martínez.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: However, Rafael and Tatiana end up developing feelings for each other, leading Tatiana to ask for a divorce.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: When Berlin discovers that she has been cheating on him with his own son, he is heartbroken and devastated.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: In a rage, he trashes the bar in a hotel, leading to his first ever arrest and mugshot.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He denies Tatiana and Rafael his inheritance.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin has short, dark brown hair with brown eyes.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: When not donning his red jumpsuit, Berlin dresses formally, often wearing suits and ties or waistcoats.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin is believed to be arrogant, narcissistic, and deemed to be a psychopath by his fellow crew, but he is shown to be extravagantly elegant, professional and charming.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He is calm, patient as well as eloquent and plays mind games with some of the hostages.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: It is said that he has a pathological need to make a good impression.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He has a sarcastic sense of humor, often mocking his fellow crew members and the hostages.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Despite this, he greatly respects his fellow crew members and will do everything to save them, as shown in the finale of Part 2.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Due to his disease, he is depressed from time to time and even suicidal in the end.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin has been married 5 times, however none of them worked out.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He describes these marriages as 5 times that he believed in love.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He stopped believing in love because he felt that once a child is born, it becomes the mother's centre of attention and that the woman is no longer "sexy".

title: Berlin
sentence_text: During the heist at the Royal Mint of Spain, Berlin meets Ariadna Cascales, an employee of the mint who is taken hostage.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Early in the heist, when Ariadna trembles in fear, Berlin approaches her to tell her than he is not a monster.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Later, when she and four other girls are sent to a room and she witnesses her fellow hostages being intimidated and taken away, Ariadna begins to panic.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Fearing for her life, she asks to speak privately with Berlin.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: In an office, she "seduces" him, thinking that it will increase her odds of surviving the heist.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: They regularly have sex, which Ariadna describes to Mónica as rape because of her hatred for him despite giving her consent.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin provides her with tranquilizers.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Ariadna confesses that Berlin has plans to marry her, but she only intends to stay with him for survival and to get a portion of the heist money.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: [7] Nairobi later reveals this information to Berlin.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: During the final shootout with police, Berlin forces Ariadna to stay with him while he guards the tunnel that the other crew members are escaping from.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin dies during the shootout and it is assumed that Ariadna was rescued.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Tatiana and Berlin get married in Berlin's Wedding.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: During the wedding, he sings "Ti Amo".

title: Berlin
sentence_text: They both also manage to steal Viking Gold at Denmark by using the concert as a distraction.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: But they broke up when he found out that his son is dating his wife.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Due to smashing up the cocktail bar where he confronted his son he was arrested.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Out of all the people in the crew, Berlin is one of the people that Tokyo disliked the most.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: She had defied and challenged him on multiple occasions throughout the show, even going so far as to hold him at gunpoint to get him to do what she wanted.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: The height of her aggression with Berlin was when she played Russian Roulette with him in the bathroom, which drove Berlin to tie her to a cart and push her out of the Royal Mint.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Tokyo describes Berlin as a shark in a swimming pool.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Nairobi is often in conflict with Berlin and disagrees with his methods.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Palermo was in love with his best friend Berlin for ten years, so much so that he suffered severely from depression after his death, turning to alcohol as a coping mechanism.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: They had met prior to the show's events, and the two came up with the Bank of Spain heist together.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: One evening, after talking to his brother about the Bank of Spain heist, Berlin went into Palermo's room and woke him up to tell him that he told Sergio about the plan, and that he thought it was amazing.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: The two then go to tell Sergio about their plan to get the gold out.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Some time before the Royal Mint of Spain heist, Berlin admits to knowing that Palermo loves him, which Palermo brushes off.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin confesses that he's never felt anything with any of the women he's been with that's remotely similar to what he has with Palermo.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He calls them soulmates, but only 99% as he likes women a lot, and the 1% defines his desire.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Palermo questions what's 99% to 1%, and then  kisses Berlin, before asking him if he's a coward.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin then pushes him against a wall, and they passionately kiss, before Berlin parts with him and says that he would do anything to feel "this".

title: Berlin
sentence_text: He professes his love to Palermo, before saying how his brother is right and that they have to part ways and scrap the plan.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Before leaving, he says that time will eventually bring them back together.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: During an argument, Nairobi accuses Palermo of being so weak that he has never been able to tell Berlin that he is in love with him.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: Berlin and the Professor are brothers and have a close relationship.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: In a flashback of Berlin and the Professor in Italy, Berlin reveals that he has Helmer's Myopathy, a condition that his mother had as well.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: The doctors predicted that he would have 3 years to live, but Berlin is hopeful that he will have 6 or 7 years to live.

title: Berlin
sentence_text: The Professor immediately decides to call off the heist and find treatment for him abroad, but Berlin refuses as there is no cure and he preferred to continue with his passion - robbing.

Silene Oliveira, better known by her code-name Tokyo (  Spanish:   Tokio ), was one of the main protagonists of the Netflix series Money Heist, as well as the narrator of the story.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She was a member of the heist crew set to rob the Royal Mint of Spain.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: 38 (season 3)

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: At the age of 14, Tokyo joined her 28 year old boyfriend into a world of crime.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Together, they successfully pulled off 15 heists.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: One of Tokyo's heists with her boyfriend involving a Prosegur pickup truck ended in a shootout that killed three people, including her boyfriend.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: From then on, she became a fugitive.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Posters with her face were put up all over Spain.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: While on her way home to see her mother for the last time before embarking on a ship, she was stopped by The Professor, who warned her that her mother's house was surrounded by police.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: He offered her to join his heist, along with 7 other robbers.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Haunted by the death of her ex-boyfriend, she vows to not mix heists with love.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: However, she quickly falls for Rio, a young and tech-savvy robber.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: In Part 5, Tokyo is shot during a shootout with soldiers.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Due to her injuries, she is unable to escape into the dumbwaiter, where the other robbers are waiting for her.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She ends up being shot multiple times and and lays on the floor while the soldiers approach her.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Gandía stands over Tokyo to mock her, so in a final act of defiance, she detonates the grenades, killing Gandía, Salvador Torrecilla, Lucas Hernando, Isaac Cobo, along with herself.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Tokyo has brown hair and brown eyes Most of the time, her hair is rather unkempt and of medium length, with the only part seemingly getting shaven or cut being her bangs, which are cut in a straight way as not to cover her eyes, and nothing more.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: On some occasions, she is seen with a different haircut, such as a bun.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Tokyo is reckless and impulsive, she usually acts before she thinks, which sometimes results in things she regrets.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She let her relationship with Rio complicate the plans for the heist.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She is quick to lose her patience, as well as sometimes being shown to quickly draw conclusions.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She can’t control her emotions very well and also expresses them intensely.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She was the first to begin worrying when The Professor did not respond to the check-up calls, and almost immediately began taking extreme measures.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She is, however, shown to care deeply for those whom she loves, as shown as when Rio is shot at the beginning of the show, and when she finds out her mother passed away.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She can be very protective and loving with the people she truly cares about.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Tokyo is confident and doesn't mind using her looks and sexuality to her advantage.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Rio begins a romantic relationship with Tokyo during their time at the Toledo Estate.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Rio and Tokyo's relationship jeopardised on the Royal Mint of Spain heist, as it affected their behavior and caused them to make impulsive decisions.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: After the first heist, they eloped together and spent time hiding on an island in the Caribbean.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: However, Tokyo eventually felt bored and desired to go to the city.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: When Tokyo forgets to call Rio, he stresses and repeatedly calls her.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Unbeknownst to Rio, the cell phone was registered to him and his repeated calls allowed Interpol to trace his location, resulting in his arrest.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: At the Bank of Spain Heist, Rio and Tokyo get through thick and thin.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Even Rio tried to save Tokyo but fails.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: René, the man Tokyo started to dock with, traveled with and enjoyed before Silene Oliveira knew that things sometimes go badly wrong.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Tokyo flirts with Denver in Part 4, after both of them have been broken up with.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Rio confronts Denver about his feelings for Tokyo.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: Out of all the people in the crew, Berlin is one of the people that Tokyo disliked the most.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: She had defied and challenged him on multiple occasions throughout the show, even going so far as to hold him at gunpoint to get him to do what she wanted.

title: Tokyo
sentence_text: The height of her aggression with Berlin was when she played Russian Roulette with him in the bathroom, which drove Berlin to tie her to a cart and push her out of the Royal Mint.

title: Tokyo

title: Tokyo

sentence_text: The two women open up and Nairobi reveals that she plans to get her son back after the heist.

The Bank of Spain (  Spanish:   Banco de España ) is the central bank of Spain.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: It is the location of the Bank of Spain Heist, shown in Parts 3, 4 and 5.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: In Welcome to the Spectacle of Life, Sagasta tells the soldiers and police that the building is blast resistant, so there are few options for breaking in, which includes the main entrance, loading docks and windows.

links.anchor_text: Welcome to the Spectacle of Life

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: César Gandía proposes another plan for entry.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: He explains that the rooftop is marked as "blast resistant" in the plans, but part of it (from the museum to steel doors) isn't due to a load bearing issue.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: The plan is for the soldiers to blow the roof off and enter the bank through the dining hall.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: However, Ángel Rubio opposes this idea.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: According to him, the coffered ceiling of the bank goes back to the 18th century.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: During the civil war, it was brought to Madrid from a place in Jaén to protect it from bombings.

title: Bank of Spain
sentence_text: There are also Goya paintings and Quimbaya gold inside.
